name: OIDC Authentication
description: OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication plugin for Mostlymatter/Mattermost. Enables SSO login via any standard OIDC provider (Keycloak, Authentik, Authelia, Dex, etc.) without requiring an Enterprise license.

settings_schema.header: Configure OpenID Connect (OIDC) authentication for your Mattermost / Mostlymatter instance. This plugin enables SSO login via any standard OIDC-compliant provider.
settings_schema.footer: After saving, restart Mattermost / Mostlymatter for changes to take effect. Redirect URI for your OIDC provider: <your-mattermost-url>/plugins/mattermost-oidc/oauth2/callback — Note: Mattermost's built-in MFA is bypassed by OIDC login. Enforce MFA at your OIDC provider instead.

settings.help_text: When true, users can log in via OpenID Connect.
The OIDC Issuer URL (e.g., https://idp.example.com/realms/myrealm). The plugin will automatically discover all endpoints via the well-known OpenID configuration.
The OAuth 2.0 Client ID registered with your OIDC provider.
The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret registered with your OIDC provider.
Space-separated list of OIDC scopes to request. Must include 'openid'. Typical: 'openid profile email'.
Text shown on the SSO login button.
Hex color code for the login button (e.g., #0058CC).
The OIDC claim to use as the username. Common: 'preferred_username', 'sub', 'nickname'.
The OIDC claim to use as the user's email address.
The OIDC claim to use for the user's first name.
The OIDC claim to use for the user's last name.
Automatically create accounts for users who authenticate via OIDC for the first time.

When enabled, existing accounts will be automatically linked to OIDC on first login if the email matches. Warning: this will override any existing auth provider (LDAP, SAML, etc.) for matching accounts.
Team name (slug) to add newly created users to. Leave blank to skip auto-joining.
